senses.definition: a public path for vehicles in a village, town, or city, usually with buildings, houses, etc. on its sides

examples.x: I crossed the street carefully at the pedestrian crosswalk.
The street was filled with colorful houses and blooming flowers.
They walked down the busy street, lined with shops and restaurants.

senses.definition: a wide street in a town or city, typically with trees on each side or in the middle

examples.x: They took a leisurely stroll down the tree-lined boulevard, enjoying the shade on a sunny day.
The grand boulevard was flanked by ornate buildings and manicured gardens.
She admired the elegant architecture along the historic boulevard.
They drove along the bustling boulevard, passing shops and restaurants.
He rode his bike down the bike lane of the boulevard, enjoying the scenic views.

senses.definition: a road designed primarily for bicycles, with features that prioritize cycling over motor vehicle traffic

examples.x: Bicycle boulevards often have lower speed limits and traffic calming measures to enhance safety for cyclists.
Cyclists appreciate bicycle boulevards for their reduced car traffic, making them more enjoyable and safer to ride on.
Cities increasingly invest in bicycle boulevards as part of efforts to promote sustainable transportation options.
Proper signage and markings along bicycle boulevards help guide cyclists and alert drivers to their shared use of the road.
The city's new bicycle boulevard includes designated bike lanes, improved crossings, and traffic signals tailored to cyclists' needs.

senses.definition: a road that combines features of a street and an avenue, mainly used in some U.S. cities such as Tucson, Arizona
the main street or avenue in a town or city, often bustling with activity

examples.x: The main drag was lined with shops and restaurants.
She enjoyed walking down the main drag on weekends.
He parked his car on the main drag before heading into the store.
They decorated the main drag with festive lights for the holidays.
The main drag was closed for a parade.

senses.definition: the most important street with many shops and stores in a town

examples.x: The new bakery opened on Main Street last week.
She strolled down Main Street, admiring the holiday decorations.
Many small businesses thrive on Main Street.
He parked his car along Main Street and walked to the diner.

senses.definition: a smaller road or street that intersects with a main road, often providing access to residential or commercial areas

examples.x: We parked on a side street to avoid the busy main road.
The restaurant is located on a quiet side street off the main boulevard.
The side street was blocked due to construction, causing a traffic detour.
The office building is on a side street, not far from the city center.
He turned onto a side street to find a shortcut to his destination.

senses.definition: a main thoroughfare or primary road in a town or city

examples.x: Fore Street is lined with various shops and cafes, offering a bustling atmosphere throughout the day.
Many tourists flock to Fore Street to explore its historic architecture and quaint charm.
The local market on Fore Street is renowned for its fresh produce and handmade crafts.
Residents often stroll along Fore Street to run errands or meet friends at nearby establishments.
Traffic can be heavy on Fore Street during rush hour, causing occasional delays for commuters.

senses.definition: an advanced roadway design that aims to improve traffic flow and safety by reducing conflict points
a road, street, or passage that provides a direct route or passage for vehicles, pedestrians, or both

examples.x: The highway serves as the main thoroughfare between the two cities.
The town's market is located along a busy thoroughfare, attracting lots of visitors.
Pedestrians were crossing the thoroughfare as cars slowed to a stop.
The new road will become a major thoroughfare for people traveling to the airport.
They live just off the main thoroughfare, so it's easy for them to get around.

senses.definition: a street or road where traffic is allowed to flow in only one direction

examples.x: They turned onto a one-way street to avoid traffic.
She checked the signs to confirm it was a one-way street.
He drove carefully on the narrow one-way street.
The delivery truck couldn't enter the one-way street from that direction.
The one-way street was part of the city's traffic management plan.

senses.definition: the most important street with a lot of shops and businesses in a town

examples.x: High Street was bustling with shoppers on Saturday morning.
She rented a flat near High Street to stay close to the shops.
The parade will pass through High Street this afternoon.
Tourists often visit High Street for unique souvenirs.
Many small businesses on High Street struggled during the economic downturn.

senses.definition: not on a public street; typically refers to parking or facilities located away from the main road

examples.x: They found off-street parking behind the building.
She preferred off-street parking for its convenience.
He parked in an off-street lot near the office.
The off-street entrance was around the corner.

senses.definition: a road designed primarily for pedestrians, where vehicles are allowed but must move slowly and yield to people on foot

examples.x: The city council implemented several living streets downtown to encourage walking and reduce traffic congestion.
Living streets typically feature wide sidewalks, benches, and trees to create a pleasant environment for pedestrians.
Residents enjoy the peaceful atmosphere of the living street where cars are secondary to pedestrians.
In Europe, many historic towns have converted their narrow alleys into charming living streets to preserve their cultural heritage.
Walking along the living street, I noticed the absence of traffic noise, making it a relaxing experience compared to busy thoroughfares.

senses.definition: a divided highway designed for high-speed traffic, typically with multiple lanes and limited access points

examples.x: They drove on the expressway to reach the city center quickly.
The expressway had a higher speed limit compared to regular roads.
She took the expressway to avoid traffic congestion on surface streets.
They installed electronic toll booths on the expressway for faster payment processing.
The expressway was well-maintained, with smooth pavement and clear signage.

senses.definition: a street that intersects with another street, usually at right angles

examples.x: They waited at the corner for traffic on the cross street to clear.
She crossed the cross street to reach the park.
He looked both ways before crossing the busy cross street.
The cross street was closed for construction.
The intersection had traffic lights for both the main street and the cross street.

senses.definition: a marked place where people walk across a street

examples.x: Make sure to use the crosswalk when crossing the street for safety.
There's a crosswalk right in front of the park, so it's easy to get across.
The car stopped at the crosswalk to let the pedestrians pass.
She looked both ways before stepping onto the crosswalk.
The police officer reminded drivers to yield to pedestrians at the crosswalk.

senses.definition: a designated area on a road where pedestrians have the right of way to cross the street safely

examples.x: Drivers must stop at the pedestrian crossing when people are waiting to cross.
He waited patiently at the pedestrian crossing for the signal to change.
The traffic light at the pedestrian crossing turned red for cars.
The pedestrian crossing was painted with bright white lines for visibility.
She looked both ways before stepping onto the pedestrian crossing.

senses.definition: a place where one is able to safely cross something, particularly a street

examples.x: The children waited at the crossing for the traffic light to turn green.
The city installed new signs to improve safety at the railway crossing.
He stopped his car to allow pedestrians to pass at the crossing.

senses.definition: a crossing or intersection of paths or roads

examples.x: The pedestrian xing was marked clearly with bright yellow lines.
Please wait for the signal before crossing at the xing.
The xing of the hiking trails in the forest made navigation easier.
At the xing of two major highways, traffic often slows down during rush hour.
The city council approved funds for improving safety measures at the railway xing.

senses.definition: a pathway typically made of concrete or asphalt at the side of a street for people to walk on

examples.x: She walked along the sidewalk on her way to the bus stop.
The city plans to repave the sidewalk near the park.
The dog pulled its owner off the sidewalk and onto the grass.
He found a quiet spot to sit on the sidewalk and enjoy his coffee.
The sidewalk was crowded with pedestrians during rush hour.

senses.definition: a raised or painted area in the middle of a road that separates lanes or controls traffic flow

examples.x: Pedestrians waited on the traffic island to cross the street.
She noticed flowers planted on the traffic island.
He drove around the traffic island to merge into the next lane.
They installed new signs on the traffic island to improve visibility.
The traffic island had a statue commemorating a local historical figure.

senses.definition: a road providing access to another road or to a specific place

examples.x: The access road to the new shopping center is still under construction.
There is a sign for an access road leading to the park entrance.
We took the access road to avoid the heavy traffic on the main highway.
The access road was blocked due to a fallen tree, causing delays for commuters.
The fire trucks used the access road behind the building to get to the scene.

senses.definition: a shopping center where stores and businesses are arranged in a row along a main thoroughfare

examples.x: They visited the strip mall to buy groceries.
She liked the convenience of the strip mall near her neighborhood.
He owned a small boutique in the strip mall.
The strip mall had a variety of shops and restaurants.
They renovated the façade of the strip mall to attract more customers.

senses.definition: a highway that encircles a city or metropolitan area, providing a route for traffic bypassing the city center

examples.x: They took the beltway to avoid downtown traffic.
She commuted daily on the beltway to reach her office.
He exited the beltway to get gas at the next exit.
The beltway had multiple lanes to accommodate heavy traffic.
They expanded the beltway to reduce congestion during rush hour.

senses.definition: a region at the edge of a city or town, typically beyond the more densely populated areas

examples.x: The new shopping mall was built in the outer belt to avoid the crowded city center.
Many commuters prefer to live in the outer belt because it offers more space and greenery.
The outer belt is known for its quiet neighborhoods and larger homes.
Developers are planning to expand public transportation to the outer belt to better serve its residents.
Schools in the outer belt often have larger campuses and more outdoor facilities.

senses.definition: a road that goes round a city or town rather than going through the city center
a major road or highway that carries a large volume of traffic between areas

examples.x: The arterial road was busy with commuters in the morning.
She avoided the arterial road during peak traffic times.
He used the arterial road to travel across the city.
The arterial road had traffic lights at every intersection.
They widened the arterial road to improve traffic flow.

senses.definition: a road running parallel to a main road, providing access to properties along the main road and serving as a service road

examples.x: They drove on the frontage road to reach the shopping center.
She exited the highway onto the frontage road.
He parked his truck on the frontage road near the construction site.
The frontage road had designated exits for local businesses.
They repaved the frontage road to improve driving conditions.

senses.definition: a pair of parallel, one-way streets that run in opposite directions to help manage traffic flow in urban areas
a section of a highway that passes through the center of a town or city, providing access to local businesses and services
a road close to a highway or major road that provides access to properties and allows local traffic to bypass the main road

examples.x: They used the service road to access the industrial park.
She drove on the service road to avoid highway traffic.
He saw the service road sign indicating the next exit.
The service road had speed limits lower than the highway.
They repaved the service road to fix potholes.

senses.definition: a main road that goes through an area and connects with other main roads
a road designed to accommodate safe and convenient use by all travelers, including pedestrians, cyclists, and motorists

examples.x: Many cities are redesigning their main thoroughfares to include bike lanes and wider sidewalks, aiming to transform them into complete streets.
The concept of a complete street emphasizes accessibility for all users, promoting healthier and more sustainable transportation options.
Engineers and urban planners collaborate to ensure that each newly constructed road adheres to the principles of a complete street.
Residents appreciate the benefits of living near a complete street, which often sees reduced traffic accidents and increased foot traffic for local businesses.
Governments across the globe are adopting policies that prioritize the development of complete streets to enhance urban mobility and safety.

senses.definition: a type of city design where streets run at right angles to each other, forming a pattern of squares or rectangles
an area in which people leave their vehicles

examples.x: I had to park in the back of the parking lot because all the spots near the entrance were taken.
They just finished repaving the parking lot, and it looks much better now.
I forgot where I parked, so I had to walk around the parking lot until I found my car.
The parking lot outside the grocery store is always crowded on weekends.
We found a spot in the parking lot right next to the entrance, which was super convenient.

senses.definition: an area in city or town where vehicles are not allowed, and people can walk freely

examples.x: The city council decided to convert the busy downtown street into a pedestrian mall to encourage more foot traffic and improve safety.
Tourists often flock to the pedestrian mall to enjoy outdoor cafes and street performers without worrying about cars.
Local businesses in the pedestrian mall have reported an increase in sales since the area became car-free.
The pedestrian mall is lined with benches and flower beds, creating a pleasant environment for shoppers and residents alike.
Residents enjoy the convenience of the pedestrian mall, where they can stroll and shop without the noise and pollution of vehicles.

senses.definition: to convert an area into one where only pedestrians are allowed, typically by closing it to vehicles

examples.x: Many cities around the world have pedestrianized their downtown areas to reduce traffic congestion and promote walking.
The city council decided last year to pedestrianize the main street to make it safer for shoppers and tourists.
By next summer, they will have pedestrianized the entire waterfront, creating a pleasant promenade for locals and visitors alike.
The town had already pedestrianized its historic square before I moved there, which made it a charming place to stroll around.
If they had pedestrianized the neighborhood years ago, perhaps it wouldn't be so difficult to find parking there now.

senses.definition: located or occurring on a public street

examples.x: They parked their car on the on-street parking space.
She enjoyed dining at the on-street café.
He walked along the on-street bike lane.
The city installed new benches on the on-street sidewalk.
